_comment: Loanword phonological EXCEPTIONS whose bound form the plain defaults get wrong. Kept in
one documented file so each per-word fact is auditable against TDK. Two attribute classes:
1. `gemination` (ünsüz ikizleşmesi): an Arabic-origin loan whose final consonant DOUBLES
before a vowel-initial suffix. hak->hakkı, af->affı, his->hissi, hac->haccı, zan->zannı,
zam->zammı, hat->hattı, sır->sırrı, üs->üssü. `ret` and `tıp` also VOICE as they double
(gemination + voicing -> redd-/tıbb-): reddi, redde, tıbbı, tıbba (tıp->tıbbı is medicine).
Before a
CONSONANT suffix the single free form is kept: hakta, afta, histe (the analyzer switches
to the doubled bound form only for a vowel-initial suffix). So `hakı` (single k) is
unrepresentable, and `hakta` (not *hakkta) is the only locative.
2. `front_harmony`: a back-SPELLED loan that HARMONIZES as if front (its historical vowel
was front). The final vowel is read through core.alphabet.FRONT_COUNTERPART for the
first suffix vowel; later suffixes inherit front harmony from the emitted front vowel.
kalp->kalbi/kalbe (also `voicing`: p->b before a vowel, but kalpte keeps p), rol->rolü/
role/roller, kabul->kabulü, usul->usulü/usulüne, meşgul (ADJ)->meşgulüm. `saat` carries
the same flag but lives in nouns.json (it was already there): saati/saate/saatte.
`hal` (state/condition) is front-harmony: hali/hale/halde/haline — a PLAIN entry would
wrongly give back-harmony *halı (colliding with halı 'carpet') / *halda, so the flag is
mandatory, not optional coverage.
3. `hukuk` is the control case: NO attribute. Voicing is OPT-IN in this engine, so a plain
entry already gives hukuku/hukuka and blocks *hukuğu by construction — no flag needed.
Verified against TDK: kalp voices (kalbi) but does NOT stay a stop before a vowel; rol/
kabul/usul do NOT voice (rolü not *rolğü); ret both geminates and voices; hak geminates
WITHOUT voicing (hakkı, never *hağğı). Each surface is pinned in tests/test_loanwords.py.
